query: What project am I working on right now?
expected_content:
What's the project I'm working on this week?

You're driving the Kubernetes migration project — moving the legacy on-prem services to GKE Autopilot. Started 3 weeks ago, currently mid-rollout.

query: Do I prefer dark mode or light mode?
expected_content:
What UI theme do I always set up?

You strongly prefer dark mode everywhere — VS Code with One Dark Pro, terminal with Dracula, even your iOS apps stay in dark.

query: When is my next doctor's appointment?

What did I have scheduled with the doctor?

Annual physical with Dr. Patel scheduled for next Wednesday at 9:30am. Bring your insurance card and the supplement list.

query: What are the flight options for my Japan trip?
expected_content:
Planning my trip to Japan — flight options?

ANA direct from SFO to NRT, 11h, ~$1400 economy. United also direct, slightly cheaper. Book 6+ weeks out for best fares.

query: Where should I stay in Tokyo?
expected_content:
Where should I stay in Tokyo?

Shibuya for nightlife and food, Shinjuku for shopping, Asakusa for traditional vibe. Recommend a hotel near a JR Yamanote line stop.

query: How do I get PyTorch working with my GPU?
expected_content:
How do I set up PyTorch with CUDA on the new GPU box?

Install pytorch with the cu124 wheel, verify with torch.cuda.is_available(). The driver should already be 550+ for CUDA 12.4 support.

query: What was that Italian cheese and pepper pasta recipe?
expected_content:
Save my favorite pasta recipe.

Cacio e pepe: 200g spaghetti, 100g pecorino romano finely grated, 1 tsp coarse black pepper. Toast pepper, emulsify cheese with starchy pasta water.

query: How do I set up credential handling in my Go project?
expected_content:
Set up authentication and credentials in my Go backend

Use chi router + jwt middleware. Validate Firebase ID tokens server-side via firebase-admin-go. Store refresh tokens in Postgres with rotation. Rate-limit auth endpoints with httprate.

query: Notes about measuring performance in Go
expected_content:
What's the best way to benchmark Go performance?

Use go test -bench=. -benchmem with table-driven benchmarks. Profile with pprof, compare runs with benchstat. For HTTP handlers, use vegeta or k6 for load.

query: Tell me about my sister who lives in Brooklyn
expected_content:
Tell me about my sister

Your sister Maya is two years older, lives in Brooklyn, works as a UX designer at a fintech startup. You text her constantly and visit a couple times a year.

query: What machine learning topics have I discussed?
expected_content:
How do I set up PyTorch with CUDA on the new GPU box?

Install pytorch with the cu124 wheel, verify with torch.cuda.is_available(). The driver should already be 550+ for CUDA 12.4 support.

---

Explain how transformers handle long context

They use self-attention so every token can attend to every other token. For long context they use techniques like sliding window attention, FlashAttention, or KV cache compression.

query: What recipes and cooking tips have I saved?
expected_content:
Save my favorite pasta recipe.

Cacio e pepe: 200g spaghetti, 100g pecorino romano finely grated, 1 tsp coarse black pepper. Toast pepper, emulsify cheese with starchy pasta water.

---

How do I make crispy chicken thighs?

Pat dry, salt 30 min ahead, sear skin-side down in cold cast iron over medium heat for 12-15 min until skin releases. Flip, finish in 425F oven for 8 min.

query: Everything about my upcoming trip to Japan
expected_content:
Planning my trip to Japan — flight options?

ANA direct from SFO to NRT, 11h, ~$1400 economy. United also direct, slightly cheaper. Book 6+ weeks out for best fares.

---

Where should I stay in Tokyo?

Shibuya for nightlife and food, Shinjuku for shopping, Asakusa for traditional vibe. Recommend a hotel near a JR Yamanote line stop.

---

What about a JR rail pass for Japan?

Worth it if you're doing Tokyo + Kyoto + Osaka. 7-day pass is ~$340. Buy before you arrive — much pricier in Japan.

query: When am I flying to Tokyo?

stm_context.content: Hey Ditto, I'm thinking about switching our backend from Node to Go
That's a big move! Go has great concurrency primitives and compiles to a single binary. What's driving the switch?
Mostly performance. Also I need to book my flight to Tokyo for next month
Exciting trip! When are you thinking of going?
March 15th through the 22nd. Need to find hotels too
Got it — a week in Tokyo starting March 15th. Want me to help research neighborhoods to stay in?
Sure, but first — what do you think about using chi for the Go router?
Chi is solid — lightweight, idiomatic, and has good middleware support. It's one of the most popular choices alongside Gin and Echo.
Cool. I also need to renew my passport before the trip
Good call — make sure you have at least 6 months validity from your travel date. You can do expedited renewal at a passport agency if you're tight on time.
Yeah I'll handle that this week. Back to Go — how's the testing story?
Go's built-in testing is great. Table-driven tests are the standard pattern, and you get benchmarking and coverage built into the toolchain.

query: What's my sister's name again?

stm_context.content: I had a great weekend visiting my sister Maya in Brooklyn
That sounds lovely! How was the visit?
Really fun. We tried this new ramen place in Williamsburg
Nice — Brooklyn has an amazing food scene. Was the ramen good?
Incredible. The tonkotsu broth was perfect. Maya wants to go back next week
Sounds like a winner! Tonkotsu is hard to beat when it's done right.
Speaking of food, I've been meal prepping more lately
That's great for saving time and money. What have you been making?
Mostly chicken and rice bowls, sometimes stir fry
Classic meal prep staples. You could mix it up with different sauces and marinades to keep it interesting.
Good idea. Also I need to buy Maya a birthday present soon
When's her birthday? Any ideas on what she'd like?
It's in March. She's really into ceramics lately

query: What was that pasta recipe I just told you about?

stm_context.content: I finally nailed cacio e pepe last night!
Nice! That's a tricky one to get right. What was your technique?
The key was using the starchy pasta water to emulsify the pecorino. I used about 200g of spaghetti and 100g of finely grated pecorino romano
That ratio sounds perfect. The starchy water trick is essential — it creates that creamy sauce without any cream.
Exactly. I also toasted the black pepper in the pan first for extra flavor
Toasting the pepper really brings out the oils and gives it a more complex flavor. Classic technique.
Anyway, switching topics — have you heard about the new M4 MacBook Pro?
Yes! The M4 chips look impressive. The Pro and Max variants especially shine for ML workloads and video editing.
I might upgrade. My M1 is starting to feel slow for Docker builds
The M4 Pro would be a solid upgrade for Docker. The extra cores and memory bandwidth make a big difference for container workloads.
How much RAM should I get?
For Docker-heavy work, 32GB minimum. If you're running multiple containers or doing ML work, 48GB gives you more headroom.
That's what I was thinking. 48GB it is

query: What was that project I mentioned earlier?

stm_context.content: The Kubernetes migration project is really picking up steam
That's great to hear! How's the progress going?
We've moved about 60% of the legacy services to GKE Autopilot so far
Nice pace! GKE Autopilot is a good choice — it handles a lot of the node management automatically.
Yeah, it's been smooth. The biggest challenge is the stateful services
Stateful workloads are always the trickiest part of a migration. Are you using StatefulSets or moving state to managed services?
Managed services where possible. We moved the PostgreSQL instances to Cloud SQL
Smart move. Cloud SQL handles backups, replication, and failover for you. Less operational burden.
Oh also, I started learning piano last week
That's awesome! Are you taking lessons or self-teaching?
Self-teaching with an app called Simply Piano. It's pretty good
Simply Piano is a popular choice. Consistent practice is key — even 15 minutes a day makes a difference.
Yeah, I'm trying to do 20 minutes every morning before work
Morning practice is great — your mind is fresh and it sets a positive tone for the day.

query: What hotel area did I decide on for my trip?

stm_context.content: I need to finalize my Tokyo hotel. I'm torn between Shibuya and Shinjuku
Both are great areas! Shibuya has more nightlife and trendy restaurants, while Shinjuku has amazing shopping and is a major transit hub.
I think Shibuya suits me better. I want to be near the food scene
Great choice! Shibuya has everything from tiny ramen shops to high-end dining. Plus the Shibuya crossing is iconic.
Can you help me debug this React component? It's not re-rendering
Sure! Common causes are mutating state directly instead of creating new references, or missing dependency arrays in useEffect.
Ah, I think it's the dependency array. I forgot to include the prop
That'll do it! React won't know to re-run the effect if the dependency isn't listed. Add the prop and it should work.
Fixed it, thanks! Also, I need to get travel insurance for the Japan trip
Definitely recommended for international travel. World Nomads and SafetyWing are popular options for tech workers.
I'll look into SafetyWing. How long do you think I should budget per day in Tokyo?
For comfortable travel: $100-150/day covers food, transport, and activities. More if you want upscale dining or shopping.
That's reasonable. I'll budget $150/day to be safe
Good plan. That gives you plenty of flexibility for spontaneous experiences.
Alright, back to coding. How do I handle errors in async Rust?
Use the ? operator with Result types. For async, tokio is the standard runtime. The anyhow crate is great for application-level error handling.

query: What programming language am I learning on the side?

stm_context.content: I've been learning Rust on weekends. The borrow checker is tough but makes sense
Rust's ownership model is one of its best features. Once it clicks, you'll appreciate the safety guarantees.
Do you know a good dentist near downtown Austin?
I don't have specific recommendations, but Yelp and Google Maps reviews are helpful. Look for one with good reviews for cleanings.
Fair enough. What's the weather going to be like this weekend?
I don't have access to real-time weather data, but you can check weather.gov or a weather app for Austin forecasts.
I'm thinking about getting a standing desk. Any recommendations?
The Uplift V2 and FlexiSpot E7 are both well-reviewed. Look for one with a programmable height controller for easy switching.
My cat keeps knocking things off my current desk anyway
Ha! A standing desk might actually help with that — fewer things at cat-accessible height. Or embrace the chaos.
Mochi is relentless. She pushed my coffee off twice yesterday
Classic cat behavior! Maybe a spill-proof travel mug for your desk would save you some grief.
Good idea. Oh, I also need to renew my gym membership
Which gym are you at? Some offer better rates if you commit to an annual plan.
It's a local CrossFit box. I've been going three times a week
Three times a week is a solid frequency for CrossFit. Enough to see progress without overdoing it.
Yeah, the WODs are brutal but I love it
That's the spirit! The community aspect of CrossFit is one of its biggest strengths too.
Totally. Anyway, back to work — need to finish this PR before lunch
Good luck! Let me know if you need help with anything.
